sectionsUnavailable: Validated blood-trait model calibration and personal lab values are unavailable in this local fixture.
Clinical diagnosis, symptoms, family history, medications, and qualified professional review are unavailable.
Blood group confirmation, transfusion context, donation context, pregnancy compatibility context, treatment context, prevention context, and actionability context are unavailable.

resultRows.item: Blood Traits and Conditions validated model
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No validated package-specific blood-trait model output is present in the local fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This local run can document that the model evidence is unavailable, but it cannot turn that absence into a diagnosis, personal risk estimate, lab value, blood type, or health action.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Clinical history, symptoms, family history, medications, blood-test values, and professional interpretation are not supplied.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Genome-derived evidence alone is not enough to decide diagnosis, screening, testing, prevention, treatment, medication, supplement, diet, compatibility, emergency, or all-clear questions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: CBC, hemoglobin, platelet count, white blood cell count, clotting tests, blood glucose, cholesterol labs, transfusion context, donation context, pregnancy compatibility context, and treatment context are not supplied.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The blood references support education about limits and review context, not a standalone conclusion about lab values, symptoms, blood group compatibility, disease risk, severity, prognosis, or actionability.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF shows a one-page Blood Related table with Trait, Your Result, and Description columns. It includes sample labels such as Higher, Typical, Type B, Positive, Secretor, and Lower.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample PDF preserves the formal report structure, but its sample labels are not local lab values, blood types, disease risks, compatibility conclusions, or medical advice.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes bilirubin as a yellow compound involved in clearance of waste products.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not a local bilirubin result or diagnosis.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes blood glucose as the concentration of glucose present in blood.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not a local glucose value, diabetes diagnosis, diet guidance, or treatment recommendation.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes metabolites as intermediate products of metabolic reactions that naturally occur within cells.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not a local metabolite or lab result.

sampleRows.description: The sample includes a 40% heritable note and describes high blood pressure as force against artery walls that is too high.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not a local blood-pressure reading, diagnosis, or treatment recommendation.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes ABO as determined by presence or absence of A and B antigens on red-cell surfaces.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not the local user's blood type or compatibility result.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes the Diego system as composed of 21 blood factors or antigens.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local transfusion, pregnancy, or compatibility guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes the Duffy glycoprotein as secreted by blood cells during inflammation and as a receptor for malaria parasite.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local malaria susceptibility, inflammation status, or compatibility guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes the Kell antigen system as a target for autoimmune diseases that destroy red blood cells.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local autoimmune, transfusion, pregnancy, or compatibility guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes the Kidd glycoprotein as helping the kidney build up high urea concentration to produce urine.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not kidney-function, transfusion, or compatibility guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes the MNS antigen system as composed of 46 antigens.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not a local blood-group or compatibility result.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes Rh(D) as the second most important blood group system after ABO.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local RhD status, pregnancy compatibility, or transfusion compatibility guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes Lewis-system glycoproteins on red cells or in body fluids.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local secretor status or medical guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes HDL cholesterol as a fatty substance essential to many metabolic processes.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not a local lipid result, diet guidance, medication guidance, or treatment recommendation.

sampleRows.description: The sample says high LDL cholesterol levels in blood have been linked to heart disease.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not a local lipid result, cardiovascular risk estimate, medication guidance, or treatment recommendation.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes GHB as a depressant that slows down messages between the brain and body.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not a local neurotransmitter, safety, diagnosis, or treatment result.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes IGFBP3 as a hormone that primarily stimulates growth and may decrease blood glucose levels.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local growth, endocrine, glucose, lab, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes IL-6 as both a pro-inflammatory cytokine and an anti-inflammatory myokine.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local inflammation, immune, lab, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes transferrins as iron-binding glycoproteins that control free iron in biological fluids.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local iron status, anemia risk, supplement guidance, lab interpretation, or treatment.

sampleRows.description: The sample describes VWF as a blood glycoprotein involved in hemostasis to prevent and stop bleeding.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample output only; not local bleeding risk, clotting status, diagnosis, testing, medication, or treatment guidance.

organSystemConditionContext.plainEnglishMeaning: The safe output for this package is an evidence-availability report with observed sample-table structure. It explains which model, clinical, professional, lab, and compatibility inputs are missing and avoids medical conclusions from missing evidence or sample labels.
unsupportedClaims: Diagnosis, personal disease risk, blood type, transfusion compatibility, donation suitability, pregnancy compatibility, lab values, glucose status, cholesterol status, clotting status, bleeding risk, carrier status, variant classification, symptom status, severity, prognosis, treatment need, medication response, supplement need, diet need, screening need, testing need, prevention action, emergency action, all-clear reassurance, and clinical actionability are not supported by this fixture.
Public sample labels such as Higher, Typical, Type B, Positive, Secretor, and Lower are not local findings.
organSystemConditionContext.professionalReview: Use this as a local-agent readiness fixture only. Health, lab, blood-type, compatibility, or symptom decisions require validated test scope, relevant clinical context, laboratory confirmation when applicable, and qualified professional review.

professionalReview: Keep this output educational and non-diagnostic.
Do not use this fixture to start, stop, or change testing, screening, medication, supplements, diet, exercise, transfusion, donation, pregnancy, or treatment decisions.
Use qualified professional review and laboratory testing for health decisions, symptoms, and blood-type compatibility questions.

genotypeSummary.gene: ABO; Diego; Duffy; Kell; Kidd; MNS; Rhesus Rh(D); FUT2

Sample biomarker-like outputs include Higher, Typical, and Lower labels for blood chemistry, cholesterol, inflammatory, iron, endocrine, and hemostasis rows without lab values.

uncertainty: No calibrated model, ancestry applicability metadata, clinical context, professional review, or personal lab values are supplied.
Public sample report rows preserve output structure and citations but are not local findings.
Absence of evidence in this fixture must be reported as unavailable, not converted into low risk or reassurance.
limitations: This generated result does not include raw genome data and does not emit VCF, FASTQ, BAM, or CRAM content.
The public sample PDF's result labels are output-format evidence only and are not the local user's blood traits, blood type, lab values, compatibility status, or disease risk.

Reference resources used for boundary language: CDC Genetic Testing; MedlinePlus Genetics; MedlinePlus: Genetic Testing; MedlinePlus Genetics: Interpreting Genetic Test Results; FDA Direct-to-Consumer Tests; MedlinePlus Genetics: complex disorders; NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores; GWAS Catalog; NHLBI Blood Diseases and Disorders Education Program; MedlinePlus Blood; Blood Related sample report.
The result is a deterministic synthetic validation artifact for local-agent packaging, not a medical interpretation.
